name: Nunton
overview: Nunton is a small village about 2.5-3.3 miles south of Salisbury in Wiltshire, part of Odstock civil parish since 1934, with the small village of Bodenham just to the east. It sits in the Ebble/Avon valley countryside close to the city, with the Church of St Andrew at its heart and the Longford Park estate to the south of Bodenham. Despite its small size it retains a farm shop, pub and church.
character: A quiet, well-kept valley village close to Salisbury, with a farm shop and pub giving it more amenities than many of its size.
amenities: Bus stop with regular services to Salisbury

schools.name: Primary schools in Salisbury / Coombe Bissett area

commutes.notes: Regular buses into the city centre

prices.summary: A desirable small village close to Salisbury; period cottages and family homes in a quiet valley setting, with limited supply. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks in the Ebble and Avon valleys
The village pub and farm shop
pros: Farm shop, pub and church retained in a small village
Quiet valley setting only ~3 miles from Salisbury
cons: No school within the village (rely on Salisbury area)
No railway station; rely on Salisbury
Small with limited shopping beyond the farm shop
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet, well-served small village within easy reach of Salisbury and the hospital.
primarySupermarket: Nunton farm shop; supermarkets in Salisbury

sources.label: The Orchard Sheltered Housing, Nunton - Wiltshire Council
